name: Fort Smith Teachers
description_short: Teachers Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union offering mortgages, auto loans, credit cards, checking accounts, and investment services with competitive rates and lower fees.
description_long:
Teachers Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union operating under NCUA insurance with routing number 221475786. The institution positions itself as one of America's fastest-growing credit unions, dedicated to serving educators and their families with comprehensive financial services. The organization's stated mission emphasizes member value and community support, with a focus on building strong financial foundations. Teachers FCU describes itself as prioritizing member financial well-being over profits, which is the defining characteristic of credit union membership.

The credit union offers a broad range of personal and business financial products. Their personal banking services include Smart Checking accounts (advertised at 2.50% APY, claimed to be 14x the national average), mortgages, Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOCs) starting at 5.49% APR, auto loans, Visa credit cards, certificates of deposit, and IRAs including limited-time 4.00% APY 15-month IRA certificates. Business accounts are mentioned but not detailed on the provided website pages. Additional services include Teachers Investment Services for retirement planning and financial management workshops. Digital banking tools are highlighted as available for remote account management.

What distinguishes Teachers FCU from traditional banks is its member-owned structure, meaning profits are theoretically returned to members through better rates and lower fees rather than distributed to shareholders. The institution emphasizes personalized service through multiple channels (online chat, phone, in-person branches) and community involvement in nonprofit organizations supporting education, human services, economic development, and research. They actively promote member benefits through special offers like Teacher Appreciation Week contests and competitive certificate rates. The credit union positions itself specifically for educators and those seeking alternatives to traditional banking institutions.

Teachers FCU operates as a legitimate credit union with strong regulatory standing (NCUA-insured) and transparent operations. However, prospective members should verify that they meet membership eligibility requirements, as credit unions typically restrict membership to specific groups or communities. The advertised rates (Smart Checking at 2.50% APY, HELOC at 5.49% APR) should be confirmed as current and subject to individual credit qualification. Like all financial institutions, actual approval rates and terms depend on personal financial circumstances.
services: Smart Checking accounts with high-yield savings rates
Mortgages for home purchase and refinancing
Visa credit cards with competitive rates
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) including fixed-term certificates
Teachers Investment Services with one-on-one financial planning
Digital banking and online account management
Online chat, phone, and in-person customer support
pros: Smart Checking account offers 2.50% APY on deposits, advertised as 14x the national average
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits prioritize member value over shareholder returns
HELOC rates starting at 5.49% APR provide competitive home equity borrowing options
Limited-time IRA certificates offering 4.00% APY on 15-month terms
Lower fees compared to traditional banks due to not-for-profit status
Multiple service channels including online chat, phone, and in-person branch support
Active community involvement with charitable giving and nonprofit partnerships focused on education
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly specified on provided website pages—may restrict who can join
Advertised rates (2.50% APY, 5.49% APR) require verification of current status and may not apply to all members depending on credit qualification
Limited detail provided on business account offerings compared to personal banking services
Website does not clearly explain fee structure or specific fee comparisons to competitors
Geographic branch availability not detailed in provided content; may have limited physical locations
best_for: Educators and teachers seeking financial institutions aligned with their professional community
Savers prioritizing higher yields on deposits through certificates and checking accounts
Homeowners seeking competitive HELOC rates for home equity borrowing
Credit union members valuing member-owned, not-for-profit banking structures over traditional banks
diagnosis: Teachers Federal Credit Union is best for educators and community members seeking competitive rates and member-focused banking through a not-for-profit credit union structure. The primary caveat is confirming membership eligibility and verifying that advertised promotional rates apply to your specific financial profile and credit circumstances.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
